seed: desire vs restraint; near_vs_far; spiral_pressure; sweep; lattice against pressure_band; sealed_edges; saturated_vs_muted; diamond; love_betrayal | abstract variation request 244; target batch count 500
title: Echoes of Containment
reasoning: The emotional tone expresses the tension between desire (vivid colors, dynamic patterns) and restraint (muted hues, structured presence). The chosen palette of hot coral and blood orange represents passionate desire and warmth, while charcoal and hollow blue are grounding colors that represent restraint and borders. The main collision occurs at the center where bright, saturated colors intersect with darker tones, representing the struggle for release amidst containment. The barcode and noise patterns interplay throughout, creating rhythm and pressure as they disrupt each other, inviting a visual dialogue of emotion rather than a literal representation. This approach captures the essence of ‘overflow vs containment’ as bright clusters assert against a dim overall ambiance, with serving negative spaces enhancing the concept of restraint. Overall, the square invites viewers into a complex emotional narrative of love and loss, longing and holding back.

layout.name: last_light
layout.description: Most of the square is dim, but one small bright cluster remains active.

composition.name: overflow_vs_containment
composition.idea: Bright or hot marks exceed a boundary while cooler marks try to contain them.

referenceExample.title: Held Hunger
referenceExample.pattern: red orbit presses against a muted locked border

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
